title: ochna aquatica
intro: Ochna aquatica, also known as the water wattle, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ochna aquatica include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and relieving respiratory issues, as well as its potential to act as an anti-diabetic agent. The plant can be prepared into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world.
